title: Regulation 9, Rule 14: Petroleum Coke Calcining Operations

text: The purpose of this rule is to limit sulfur dioxide (SO 2) emissions from the thermal processing of petroleum coke.
A refractory lined cylindrical device that that rotates on its own axis to drive off contaminants from green petroleum coke by bringing the coke into contact with heated gas.
A carbonaceous solid derived from the thermal processing of petroleum products.
The thermal processing of petroleum coke through a kiln.
The owner/operator of a petroleum coke calcining operation sub ject to this rule shall comply w ith all of the following:
Effective January 1, 2019, operate all Petroleum Coke Calcinin g Kilns such that the SO 2 emissions from all kilns combined do not exceed 320 pounds per hour, averaged over any consecutive 24 hours.
Effective January 1, 2020, operate all Petroleum Coke Calcinin g Kilns such that the SO 2 emissions from all kilns combined do not exceed 1, 050 tons per year on a twelve-month rolling average basis.

sections.section_title: Continuous Emission Monitoring and Recordkeeping Requi rements
text: T h e owner/operator shall use Continuous Emission Monitors to measur e SO 2 emissions from each Petroleum Coke Calcining Kiln. The owner/operator shall be subject to the applicable Continuous Emission Monitor requirements in Regulati on 1. Effective one year from the date of adoption of this rule, the owner/operator shall maintain emission monitoring records for a period of 5 years and make them availa ble to the APCO upon request.
Effective January 1, 2019, the owner/operator of a petroleum coke calcining operation subj ect to this rule shall comply with all of the following:
Maintain annual records of the green coke processed in each kil n.
Use a calibrated APCO-approved load cell on all kilns to monit or the mass of sorbent material injected into each kiln on a per hour basis.
All records and summaries subject to this provision shall be re tained for 5 years and shall be submitted to the APCO upon request.

Any facility that operates a pet roleum coke calcining facilit y subject to Section 9-14-301 shall provide, properly install, ma intain in good working order, and operate the following emission monitoring equipment:
Continuous Emissions Monitoring: A continuous emission monito ring system

subsections.label: (CEMS)
text: for each kiln, to demonstrate compliance with the provis ion of this rule for sulfur dioxide (SOx) emissions. The CEMS shall meet the req uirements of the District Manual of Procedures, Volume V, Continuous Emissio n Monitoring, Policy and Procedures. Each CEMS shall complete a minimum of on e cycle of operation sampling, analyzing, and data recording for each succ essive fifteen (15) minute period.
